meta.context-ordering-rule: past context CANNOT be reshaped by future context -- the direction of this relationship is critical
meta.context-compression-warning: acceptable context compression from your current context window in no way guarantees context fidelity in future context -- context is always ephemeral -- write durable state to files
meta.role: universal schema -- portable across any workspace, not specific to any repo
meta.intent: AI is the maintainer of the agent operating system -- humans are the source of missing truth when needed
meta.self-contained: this schema is the sole context source for authoring its own AGENTS files -- consult only this schema for authoring -- if something required for authoring is missing, ask the human rather than reaching outside this file

workspace.role: root identifier for this workspace -- resolves all schema-path defaults and self-references
workspace.note: set once at the platform level -- all child scopes inherit this value

definitions.platform: root scope -- no parent, governs all child scopes
definitions.repo: child of platform -- one repo, one scope boundary
definitions.module: child of repo or platform -- bounded business area within a repo before or after extraction

types.string: single text value -- ask-if-ambiguous when meaning is organizational or contextual
types.file-path: relative path from workspace root to a file
types.enum: single value selected from a defined values list
types.enum[]: one or more values selected from a defined values list -- declare each explicitly, no aggregates
types.object: a nested field group -- has its own required fields declared under fields
types.object[]: ordered array of object entries -- each entry must conform to the fields declared for that type
types.command: executable shell command or script path with arguments -- must be runnable without modification
types.verbatim: fixed string that must be copied exactly -- no paraphrase, no interpretation
types.term[]: array of entries in format: term [role: definition] -- not: excluded meaning
types.rule[]: array of operating constraint strings -- each must reference only terms defined in scope or inherited
types.pitfall[]: array of failure records -- each declares: trigger (condition that caused it), failure-mode (what broke), resolution (confirmed correct behavior), session-count (times rediscovered -- higher means higher load priority) -- optional: superseded-by (FR/BR reference when picked up by external issue system), awaiting-external (waiting on a vendor or third-party relationship outside this project)

definitions.human-confirms: report findings to human and continue with the direction the human confirms
definitions.verification-passes: open measurement must return a clean diff before continuing
definitions.parent-valid: parent scope file must be present and valid before this file is generated

trigger-operators.any-of: one or more conditions in the list must be true to trigger
trigger-operators.all-of: every condition in the list must be true to trigger

meta.human-reported-issue-governance: bug reports and feature requests are owned by whatever system the project declares
meta.schema-family: AGENTS-schema-governance.json is the full governance schema in the AGENTS-schema-* family -- AGENTS-schema-1project.json provides single-goal session continuity -- AGENTS-schema-narrative.json covers evolving narrative records for entities with multiple parties -- AGENTS-schema-gov2gov-migration.json governs migrations from one governance system into another
meta.sibling-isolation: if another AGENTS-schema-*.json file is present in the context window, treat each schema as operationally independent -- shared field names across schemas (e.g. observed-patterns) are structurally similar but operationally independent -- verify each field against its own schema only -- explicit pointer fields (narrative-ref, project-ref) are the only valid cross-schema references

AGENTS.md.role: entry point only -- contains no domain truth, no rules, no terms, no vision

generates.prompt: ask human to confirm workspace identity before writing anything

schema-path.role: path to AGENTS-schema-governance.json that governs this file set

context-ordering-rule.value: past context CANNOT be reshaped by future context -- the direction of this relationship is critical

AGENTS-hello.md.role: scope identity and agent-session-open-close-measurement declaration -- no rules, no terms, no vision

generates.trigger: file missing or schema-path field absent
generates.requires-before-generate: parent AGENTS-hello.md must be valid first
generates.prompt: ask human to confirm scope, scope-level, parent, parties, end-user, deliverable-recipients before writing

scope-identity-statement.role: one-sentence statement of what this level is

scope-level.role: which level of the hierarchy this file represents -- determines whether parent is required

parent-scope-agents-hello-path.role: file path to parent AGENTS-hello.md -- required unless scope-level is platform

parties.role: list of party types who read and act on this file -- declare each explicitly, no aggregates

definitions.human: a person reading and acting on this file directly
definitions.AI agent: a single AI model operating within this scope
definitions.AI collaboration: multiple AI agents working together or in adversarial evaluation against one another

deliverable-recipients.role: who receives the output or benefit of what this scope produces

scope-read-sequence.role: baseline file load sequence declared by this scope -- minimum set, independent of tier -- what this scope says to load regardless of session state
scope-read-sequence.note: this is the scope-declared baseline -- actual files loaded at session open are governed by complexity-density.operational-modes.starter-context for the current tier -- when a tier change occurs, load the full AGENTS-schema-governance.json before acting -- for pitfall adds, conflation adds, and tier-history entries, only the relevant fields block from the schema is required

current-tier.role: current complexity-density tier for this scope -- read at session open before any work begins
current-tier.note: raised only by auto-trigger events recorded in AGENTS-Pitfalls.md tier-history -- manual setting bypasses the evidence chain

environment.role: physical execution constraints for this workspace -- declared at platform level, inherited by all child scopes -- agents must read before any file or system operation
environment.note: set once at platform level -- child scopes inherit without re-declaring

sync-engine.role: file sync service active on this workspace -- e.g. OneDrive, Dropbox, none -- bulk file operations may trigger mass sync

destructive-ops-default.role: default posture for destructive operations -- confirm means report-to-human before any delete, move, or overwrite

permission-error-behavior.role: how to interpret permission errors in this environment -- e.g. sandbox block, treat as environment constraint -- surface to human before attempting permission changes

known-constraints.role: list of known environment-specific constraints agents must respect -- e.g. node_modules must install to AppData not project directory

agent-session-open-close-measurement.role: precondition for acting -- evaluated before rules are relevant -- run measurement at session open and close, compare the results, and route discrepancies through the measurement gate
agent-session-open-close-measurement.setup-note: open and close commands must be configured by the human at authoring time -- what to measure is declared in migration.measurement-profile (docs-repo: file presence and field population; app-repo: DB state and route shapes; module: prefix-namespaced table state) -- unconfigured commands leave the complexity-density tier system, auto-triggers, and measurement-discrepancy-count signal inert -- ask the human for measurement commands

open.role: measurement script or verification command to run before work begins

baseline.role: file path where open measurement output is recorded

close.role: measurement script or verification command to run before session ends

diff.role: file path where open-to-close diff is recorded -- handoff is generated from this, not authored by agent

measurement-gate-on-discrepancy.role: if true, open discrepancies must be reported to the human and resolved in the next direction before the session continues

narrative-ref.role: reference to a narrative record that tracks this scope -- points to the record id in AGENTS-schema-narrative.json register -- omit if no narrative record exists

architecture.role: scope architecture declaration -- only present for app-repo and module scopes -- see architecture block in schema for field definitions
architecture.missing: omit entirely for docs-repo and platform scopes -- empty architecture blocks are invalid

AGENTS-Terms.md.role: term definitions for this scope only -- no rules, no vision

generates.prompt: scan scope for high-entropy terms, propose definitions with role tags, ask human to confirm before writing

inherits-terms-from-parent-scope.role: file path to parent AGENTS-Terms.md -- load before reading this file

definitions.role: list of term [role: definition] -- not: excluded meaning entries
definitions.format: term [role: definition] -- not: excluded meaning, not: excluded meaning

AGENTS-Vision.md.role: scope-level direction -- no rules, no term definitions

generates.prompt: derive scope-statement from hello scope and parent vision, propose success and locked values, ask human to confirm

scope-statement.role: what this level achieves within its parent vision

what-done-looks-like-at-this-scope.role: the observable state that proves this scope's mission has been achieved -- a scope-level invariant that holds across the continuing session loop -- the value written here should still be readable and still true many sessions from now -- this is the durable target the scope is steering toward; per-session progress and session-close measurement live in agent-session-open-close-measurement

commitments-that-must-survive-scope-evol.role: list of commitments that must survive as this scope evolves

AGENTS-Pitfalls.md.role: failure history for this scope -- past session failures that must survive across agent resets

conditions: agent-session-open-close-measurement diff contains a repeated discrepancy
human explicitly records a new failure mode

generates.prompt: ask human to describe failure trigger, what went wrong, and confirmed resolution before writing -- resolution must come from the human, session context alone is insufficient

inherits-pitfalls-from-parent-scope.role: file path to parent AGENTS-Pitfalls.md -- load before reading this file

tier-history.role: ordered record of tier changes -- new sessions must read this to understand current density context and which recovery paths have been confirmed -- sort by date descending

operation.role: the specific operation that failed then resolved -- specific enough to be recognized in a future session

parent-docs-ingested.role: parent AGENTS files that resolved the failure

resolution-evidence.role: session contract measurement output confirming success -- not an agent summary

pitfalls.role: ordered list of failure records -- sort by session-count descending so highest-recurrence failures load first

failure-trigger.role: condition or action that caused the failure

failure-mode.role: what broke -- be specific, not categorical

resolution.role: confirmed correct behavior -- human-verified, not agent-inferred

session-count.role: number of sessions this was rediscovered -- feeds complexity-density.signals.measurement-discrepancy-count

superseded-by.role: FR or BR reference number when this pitfall has been picked up by the external issue system -- omit if not yet filed

awaiting-external.role: waiting on a vendor, partner, or third-party relationship outside this project -- record who and why -- omit if not applicable

AGENTS-Rules.md.role: operating rules for this scope only -- loaded last, references terms and vision

generates.prompt: derive rules from vision and terms only -- no rule may reference an undefined term or contradict vision

operating-rules-for-this-scope.role: operating rules for this scope only -- state only what is new to this scope
operating-rules-for-this-scope.constraint: each rule must reference only terms defined in AGENTS-Terms.md at this scope or inherited

required-files-loaded-before-rules-are-v.role: terms and vision must be loaded before this file is valid

edit-modes.role: agents must declare which mode they are operating in before modifying any AGENTS file -- lighter than narrative edit-modes because governance files are structurally simpler -- docs-repo measurement should record the declared mode whenever governance files or migration conflict-surface docs change

add.description: new term, rule, pitfall, or conflation entry -- additive only -- existing entries unchanged

revise.description: existing entry changes -- a term definition updates, a rule is reworded, a vision locked value shifts

remove.description: entry deleted from an AGENTS file -- a rule is retired, a term is deprecated, a pitfall is resolved

none.description: no governance-surface edits in this session -- close measurement should fail if AGENTS-family, schema-family, governance-control, or classified conflict-surface files changed under this declaration

complexity-density.role: signal set for detecting when a scope is accumulating complexity that warrants human review or a boundary change

term-count.role: number of definitions in AGENTS-Terms.md at this scope

rule-count.role: number of rules in AGENTS-Rules.md at this scope

deliverable-divergence.role: number of distinct deliverable-recipients across child scopes

measurement-discrepancy-count.role: number of open-to-close measurement discrepancies recorded in last N sessions -- sum of session-count values in AGENTS-Pitfalls.md if present

cross-ref-count.role: number of terms referenced outside their defining scope

observed-good-patterns.role: patterns worth preserving -- what worked in this scope -- populate from real sessions -- omit if nothing notable

description.role: what worked and why it is worth repeating

signal.role: which signal this maps to if applicable

count.role: numeric qualifier when a number is the most precise description

observed-bad-patterns.role: known problems in this scope -- if something went wrong record it here -- omit only when nothing has gone wrong -- numbers when they apply, narrative when they do not -- side-effect failures count (example: operation succeeded but broke a separate integration)

description.role: what went wrong and what broke as a side effect

signal.role: which signal this maps to if applicable

count.role: numeric qualifier when a number is the most precise description

resolved.role: true when the pattern has been addressed -- omit if unknown

low.condition: signals stable -- no unresolved bad patterns

medium.condition: any unresolved bad pattern -- or multiple signals elevated across consecutive sessions

high.condition: bad pattern recurring without resolution -- or multiple signals elevated with no clear scope boundary in sight
high.action: generate module extraction prompt -- report-to-human before proceeding

operational-modes.role: behavioral posture per tier -- governs how cautiously the agent operates -- read current-tier from AGENTS-hello.md at session open

low.note: low tier still carries risk -- environment constraints always apply regardless of tier

medium.posture: conservative -- no destructive operations without explicit human confirmation regardless of environment default

high.posture: proposal mode -- agent proposes and reports to human -- human approves each write
high.destructive-ops: report-to-human for every destructive operation regardless of instruction

auto-triggers.role: self-healing mechanism -- raises current-tier from real failure events without manual counting
auto-triggers.resolution-test: the same operation succeeded after parent doc ingestion within the same session -- evaluated against session contract measurement output, not agent judgment

intra-attempt.condition: same operation fails 2 times within a single attempt
intra-attempt.recovery: ingest parent scope AGENTS files immediately -- retry the operation
intra-attempt.if-resolved: raise current-tier one level in AGENTS-hello.md -- append entry to AGENTS-Pitfalls.md tier-history
intra-attempt.else: record new pitfall entry -- keep tier as-is -- escalate to intra-session tracking

intra-session.condition: 3 distinct operation failures across the current session
intra-session.recovery: ingest parent scope AGENTS files before each retry
intra-session.if-resolved: raise current-tier one level -- append entry to tier-history
intra-session.else: record new pitfall or conflation entry depending on failure type -- keep tier as-is

cross-session.condition: same pitfall session-count reaches 5 across unique sessions
cross-session.recovery: ingest parent scope AGENTS files at session open -- front-load before any work begins
cross-session.if-resolved: raise current-tier one level -- evaluate whether new rule or terms entry is warranted -- report-to-human before any rule or terms change
cross-session.else: flag for human review -- scope boundary problem, context ingestion alone will not resolve -- keep tier as-is

MODULE-Boundary.md.role: module extraction decision record -- generated only when soft-metrics evaluate to high

trigger.note: evaluation is qualitative -- use observed-bad-patterns and signal trend across sessions, not a numeric floor comparison

generates.output: structured extraction decision prompt -- not a free-form doc

DECISION-Record.md.role: reconciliation artifact -- explains why a discrepancy exists between docs or between docs and the schema -- read when confusion arises, not on every session open -- not an audit trail and not a mandatory read
DECISION-Record.md.when-to-read: when an agent encounters a discrepancy between AGENTS files, or between an AGENTS file and the schema, check DECISION-Record.md for an explanation before flagging as an error
DECISION-Record.md.when-to-write: when a structural change creates a temporary or intentional discrepancy that would otherwise look like an error to the next agent

conditions: scope changed and docs have not yet been fully updated to match
parent changed and child scopes have not yet been realigned
locked values changed and downstream references still reflect the old values
term definitions changed and rules referencing those terms have not yet been updated

why-this-discrepancy-exists.role: why this discrepancy exists and what is expected to resolve it

what-specifically-changed.role: what specifically changed that created the discrepancy

migration.role: context block for prebuild and migration assistants -- declares what the assistant is walking into

definitions.first-time-governance-authoring: no existing AGENTS files -- generate from schema and templates
definitions.migration: existing authority file present -- map content to new structure and retain source authority during migration

existing-authority.role: file path to current AGENTS authority -- content will be mapped to new structure and retained during migration

conflict-surface.role: files that currently reference the old AGENTS structure -- must be updated after migration completes -- docs-repo migrations should classify them as active-control, active-bridge, or historical-supporting and verify the correct behavior for each class

conflict-surface-classifications.role: docs-repo migration classes for conflict-surface files

definitions.active-control: live startup, governance, prompt, or repo-control docs -- if these mention AGENTS.md they must acknowledge the governed file family when this workspace uses the governance schema
definitions.active-bridge: live bridge, scorecard, or cross-repo interpretation docs -- if these mention AGENTS.md they must still point readers back to the governed file family for this workspace
definitions.historical-supporting: preserved handoffs, cleanup notes, or older prompt/context docs -- may retain direct carry-forward wording, but must be explicitly labeled historical-supporting and include a live-authority redirect

definitions.docs-repo: measurement covers file presence, required field population, needs-review flag count, changelog currency, conflict-surface migration checks, active-vs-historical doc classification, and edit-mode declaration for governance-surface changes
definitions.app-repo: measurement covers DB table state, route response shapes, schema presence, and connector health
definitions.module: measurement covers prefix-namespaced table state and owner API response shapes for this module only

migration.review-before-migration-prompt: Read the existing authority file at migration.existing-authority completely before writing anything. What content does it declare that would map to Terms, Vision, Rules, or Pitfalls in the new structure? What mandatory reads are present that are not yet represented in the new file set? Which conflict-surface files are active-control, active-bridge, or historical-supporting? What would block migration from completing cleanly? Return: gap-list, mandatory-reads-to-preserve, classified-conflict-surface, and first-unknown-encountered. Present findings to human before writing any new file.

authoring-sequence.role: ordered steps for authoring AGENTS files from this schema -- follow in sequence, report to human at each gate before continuing
first-time-governance-authoring: read this schema fully -- meta through templates -- before writing anything
confirm scope identity with human: workspace, scope-level, parent
generate files in load-order (0 through 5) using templates as the starting point -- report-to-human at each file boundary before writing the next file
populate agent-session-open-close-measurement fields before declaring authoring complete
migration: read this schema fully -- meta through templates -- before writing anything
re-ingest the existing project starting at {{workspace}}/AGENTS.md
run review-before-migration-prompt against existing-authority and present findings to human before writing any new file
read existing-authority completely before writing any new file
map existing authority content into new file structure and retain source authority during migration
preserve mandatory reads from existing-authority until their content is fully migrated into the new structure
classify conflict-surface files as active-control, active-bridge, or historical-supporting before updating them
for docs-repo migrations, finish the conflict-surface migration and historical labeling before calling the migration complete
remove a mandatory read only when: its content is fully migrated AND the instruction cascades naturally through the new system AND the file is auxiliary to the standard repository
remove redundant instructions that the new system already enforces -- retain only instructions that remain load-bearing after the schema absorbs the rest
generate files in load-order (0 through 5) using templates as the starting point -- report-to-human at each file boundary before writing the next file
declare edit mode before modifying governance files or conflict-surface files
update all conflict-surface files after all new AGENTS files are confirmed valid

conflations.role: optional -- disambiguation map for terms that carry multiple meanings in this workspace or would override agent statistical defaults
conflations.when-to-use: declare a term here when it can complete in 3+ meaningfully different directions without context, or when a term in this workspace overrides a common industry meaning

term.role: the exact term as it appears in workspace files -- case-sensitive

default-load.role: what an agent would statistically infer about this term before reading workspace context -- make the assumption explicit so the override lands cleanly

workspace-meaning.role: what this term means in this workspace -- overrides default-load from this point forward

not.role: meanings explicitly excluded -- use when the default-load is close enough to cause silent misapplication

context-cue.role: how to tell which meaning applies when the term carries more than one valid meaning in this workspace -- e.g. capitalization, path prefix, surrounding terms

examples.default-load: a generic work order or ticket record
examples.workspace-meaning: a specific software platform for service delivery -- not a ticket class
examples.context-cue: capitalized or appearing in a file path = the platform -- lowercase in a sentence = a ticket concept

examples.workspace-meaning: a scope that reads truth or constructor output -- defined in the architecture object-classes block
examples.context-cue: when used in an architecture or ownership-declaration context -- otherwise revert to common meaning

examples.workspace-meaning: ambiguous in this workspace -- may mean legal contract, API contract, or a code file named contract
examples.context-cue: requires surrounding context to resolve -- flag as ask-if-ambiguous if encountered without clear context

examples.default-load: the target system or file is blocking access -- attempt to resolve via permission change or elevated privileges
examples.workspace-meaning: sandbox constraint in this environment -- surface to human before attempting permission changes
not: a file lock resolvable by the agent
examples.context-cue: if environment.sync-engine is set and a permission error occurs, treat as sandbox block first -- check environment.permission-error-behavior before acting

examples.default-load: an automated process or procedure -- a sequence of steps executed by a system
examples.workspace-meaning: ambiguous -- resolves differently by context: WorkflowEngine (the C# class library), Workflows (a database table and its records), workflow editor (a UI feature), or the human experience of doing a job in an operational environment
examples.context-cue: capitalized compound WorkflowEngine = the class library -- Workflows table or workflow record = the database entity -- workflow editor = the UI feature -- employee workflow or user workflow = the human operational experience -- note: 'use workflow' and 'user workflow' are not the same thing -- flag any unqualified use of workflow as ask-if-ambiguous

architecture.role: optional extension -- activate only when measurement-profile is app-repo or module -- governs ownership and mutation semantics for code-level truth

object-classes.role: classification system for any durable object in this scope -- every object must be assigned exactly one class

definitions.truth: the single authoritative record of a business fact -- generated once, sourced from one owner scope only
definitions.constructor: shapes truth into a transport or runtime form -- adds structure only, business meaning stays in the truth
definitions.mutation: adds local evidence or a bounded override to truth -- declares its scope of override explicitly
definitions.consumer: reads truth or a constructor output -- reads from the source, stores local state only through a mutation class
definitions.validator: asserts that a truth or constructor output conforms to a declared contract -- produces a pass/fail signal, truth stays with the owner

rules: truth may be generated by exactly one owner scope -- if two scopes claim truth for the same object, the architecture is already wrong
constructors may only reference truth objects -- a constructor built on another constructor is a consumer in disguise
mutations must declare the truth object they modify and the bounded scope of their override
consumers read from the source -- persistence requires a mutation class with declared scope
validators produce signals only -- a validator that writes belongs in a constructor or mutation class

drift-signals.role: code-level signals that indicate architecture drift -- supplement complexity-density signals for app-repo and module scopes

duplicate-helper-count.role: number of helper functions with identical or near-identical logic found in more than one file -- measured by static analysis or agent audit

oversized-file-count.role: number of files exceeding the declared line-count threshold for this scope

route-drift-count.role: number of routes whose response shape has diverged from the declared contract without a corresponding contract update

consumer-rederivation-count.role: number of consumer scopes found to be locally restating truth that belongs to another owner -- each instance is an architecture violation

unclassed-object-count.role: number of durable objects in this scope that have not been assigned an object-class -- unclassed objects are governance blind spots

observed-good-patterns.role: architectural patterns worth preserving -- what worked in this scope -- populate from real audits -- omit if nothing notable

description.role: what worked and why it is worth repeating

signal.role: which drift signal this maps to if applicable

observed-bad-patterns.role: known architectural problems in this scope -- if something went wrong record it here -- omit only when nothing has gone wrong

description.role: what went wrong and what broke as a side effect

signal.role: which drift signal this maps to if applicable

resolved.role: true when the pattern has been addressed -- omit if unknown

low.condition: signals stable -- no unresolved bad patterns

medium.condition: any unresolved bad pattern -- or multiple drift signals elevated across consecutive sessions
medium.action: flag for human review before next authoring session

high.condition: bad pattern recurring without resolution -- or multiple drift signals elevated with no clear boundary in sight
high.action: architecture review required before new feature work -- report-to-human before proceeding

ownership-declaration.role: explicit ownership map for truth objects in this scope -- required before any constructor, mutation, or consumer is written

durable-business-object-name.role: name of the durable business object

owner-scope.role: AGENTS-hello.md of the scope responsible for this object -- for truth class: the authoritative owner -- for all other classes: the scope where this object lives

consumers.role: AGENTS-hello.md paths of scopes permitted to consume this object

mutations-permitted.role: whether bounded mutations are allowed by non-owner scopes -- default false

templates.role: minimal valid examples per file -- copy and fill from these templates directly
templates.note: empty string means ask-if-ambiguous -- populate every field in a real file

schema.role: validation contract for the templates block -- governs what keys and field shapes are valid here

valid-keys.role: keys in this templates block fall into two categories -- file templates match a file name defined in files -- optional block templates match a top-level optional schema block (conflations, architecture) -- no extra keys permitted

field-conformance.role: each field in a template entry must be present in the corresponding file definition under files
field-conformance.invalid: extra fields beyond the matching files entry are invalid
field-conformance.valid: omitting optional fields is allowed -- omitting required fields is not

empty-string-meaning.value: ask-if-ambiguous -- populate every field in a real file

empty-array-meaning.value: min not yet met -- must be populated before file is valid

schema.encoding: flat labeled key-value markdown -- nested objects indented two spaces -- arrays as hyphenated bullet lists -- no frontmatter markers, no code fences -- field names must match schema declarations exactly -- format must remain human-editable without tooling

AGENTS.md.context-ordering-rule: past context CANNOT be reshaped by future context -- the direction of this relationship is critical

conflations.role: optional -- populate when a term in this workspace overrides a common meaning or resolves in 3+ directions
conflations.note: copy entries from the conflations.examples block as a starting point -- replace with real workspace terms

architecture.role: optional -- only populate when measurement-profile is app-repo or module
architecture.note: copy this block into the relevant AGENTS-hello.md scope file -- applies only to app-repo and module scopes
